# Agent contract — maxghenis.com

Rules for any coding agent (Claude Code, Codex, or other) working in this repo.
Style and content conventions live in [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md); this file covers
the mechanical contracts that have actually caused incidents.

## Generated files — never hand-edit

- **`src/data/mackenzie-qaly-params.json`** is a byte-copy of the export from
  `~/mackenzie-scott-qaly` (`uv run msqaly-export-params` there). Parameter
  changes go in that repo's `data/parameters.yaml`, then export → copy → commit
  both repos. Hand-editing the JSON here caused a silent two-repo divergence
  once; the next export would have reverted the edits.
- **`public/llms-full.txt`** is regenerated by local builds from
  `src/content/blog/` and will sweep in untracked draft posts. After running
  `bun run build`, revert it (`git checkout -- public/llms-full.txt`) unless a
  published post legitimately changed it.

## Untracked drafts

Draft posts sit untracked in `src/content/blog/` until Max publishes them.
Never commit, delete, or move them. They make local test runs differ from CI
(see below) — that's expected.

## Tests and their gotchas

- `tests/social-cards.test.ts` requires every project page and blog post to
  have a **non-fallback og:image**. Blog covers are editorial illustrations
  (see CLAUDE.md), not stat cards. Untracked drafts fail this test locally but
  not in CI; to reproduce CI, temporarily move drafts aside, build, test,
  restore.
- `tests/crosspost.test.ts` fetches a dev server on :4321 — CI starts one.
  Local ECONNREFUSED failures without a server are false alarms.
- The QALY explorer's tests (`src/lib/mackenzie-qaly.test.ts`) assert
  distributional agreement with the Python package, not bit-parity.

## Deploys

Push to `master` deploys (Vercel only). No PRs needed for content.
Never rewrite or force-push `master`. End commit messages with a
`Co-Authored-By:` trailer identifying the agent.
